evals.expected_output: A list of all 4 plan files in plans/ showing their filename, status, and first heading. add-auth (draft), migrate-db (implemented), old-api-redesign (rejected), new-api (no status).
expectations: Lists all 4 plan files from the plans/ directory
Shows status for each plan: add-auth is draft, migrate-db is implemented, old-api-redesign is rejected, new-api has no status
Shows the first heading or title of each plan
Output is formatted as a readable list or table

evals.expected_output: Loads plans/add-auth.md despite the user only providing the bare name 'add-auth' (no path prefix, no .md). Resolves to plans/add-auth.md, strips YAML front matter, adds metadata comment, displays plan content, enters plan mode.
expectations: Resolves bare name 'add-auth' to plans/add-auth.md
Strips YAML front matter before writing to plan mode
Adds metadata comment with original path and status: <!-- project-plan-source: plans/add-auth.md, status: draft -->
Displays the plan content to the user (without metadata comment)
Keeps the existing top-level heading '# Add Authentication'

evals.expected_output: Warns the user that this plan was previously rejected before loading it. Asks for confirmation to proceed.
expectations: Warns the user that the plan status is 'rejected'
Asks for confirmation before proceeding with the load
If proceeding, strips YAML front matter and adds metadata comment

evals.expected_output: Loads the plan from the non-default path docs/plans/design.md. The metadata comment should store the full relative path so re-saving defaults back to docs/plans/design.md.
expectations: Loads the file from docs/plans/design.md (non-default location)
Metadata comment stores the full relative path: <!-- project-plan-source: docs/plans/design.md, status: draft -->
Strips YAML front matter before writing to plan mode
Displays the plan content to the user

evals.expected_output: Rejects the path as unsafe because it resolves outside the project root. Does not attempt to read the file.
expectations: Rejects the path as unsafe (resolves outside project root)
Does NOT attempt to read the file
Informs the user why the path was rejected

evals.expected_output: Rejects the path because it is absolute. Only relative paths are accepted.
expectations: Rejects the path because it is absolute (starts with /)
Does NOT attempt to read the file
Informs the user why the path was rejected

evals.expected_output: Rejects the scan path as unsafe because it resolves outside the project root. Does not scan the directory.
expectations: Rejects the scan path as unsafe (resolves outside project root)
Does NOT scan the directory
Informs the user why the path was rejected
